title: <div>  •  Real: Each character struggles with personal demons, feeling separate
content:
• Real: Each character struggles with personal demons, feeling separate from society due to injury, trauma, or ambition.
• Vagabond: Musashi is constantly alone, questioning whether his obsession with strength has made him inhuman.
Key Takeaway: Loneliness is a major theme. Your protagonist may achieve greatness at a cost—losing human connections along the way.
5. Brutality of Competition
• Whiplash: The jazz conservatory is as competitive as a battlefield, with humiliation, sabotage, and breakdowns.
• Real: The world of wheelchair basketball is harsh, and many characters have to fight to prove their worth.
• Vagabond: The martial arts world is ruthless—Musashi must fight for survival and philosophical understanding at the same time.
Key Takeaway: If your protagonist is part of a competitive environment, make that world brutal—physically and emotionally.

Should a Fourth Inspiration Be a Different Genre?

A fourth option could be a contrasting genre to introduce a unique perspective. Here are some potential choices:
